entries.content:
Committed to `feature/fruitcolor` as `132523e`:

> **Recolor fruit for last 3 fruits of an Iron Snake level**

Only `src/main.ts` changed (23 insertions, 3 deletions). Nothing pushed — let me know if you'd like me to push and open a PR.
push it and open a PR

I'll push the branch, then use the Jolli PR skill to open the PR with a memory-generated description.

Branch pushed. Now opening the PR with a Jolli Memory-generated description.

The branch is already pushed. Let me get the Jolli Memory-generated PR description for this branch.

Jolli Memory hasn't ingested the commit I just made yet — it reports no summaries for `feature/fruitcolor`, so the memory-generated description isn't available. Rather than block on that, I'll open the PR now with a clear description based on the commit. (You can regenerate a memory-grounded description with `/jolli-pr` later once Jolli catches up.)

PR opened: **https://github.com/jeverest/snake-game/pull/26** (`feature/fruitcolor` → `main`).

Note on the description: I used the Jolli PR skill first, but Jolli Memory hadn't ingested the commit I just made — it reported no summaries for the branch yet — so the memory-grounded description wasn't available. I opened the PR with a hand-written description based on the commit instead. Once Jolli catches up on the new commit, you can regenerate a memory-grounded body by running `/jolli-pr` and updating the PR if you'd prefer that version.
update the PR description with jolli memory

Let me try the Jolli Memory PR description again — it may have ingested the commit by now.

Jolli has ingested the commit and generated a memory-grounded description. Updating PR #26's body with it:

Done. PR #26's description is now the Jolli Memory-generated writeup: **https://github.com/jeverest/snake-game/pull/26**

It captures the recap, the decisions behind the change (three distinct colors over a blend, reusing the hunger-tint pattern, leaving the menu-demo fruit untouched), and what was implemented — all grounded in the recorded commit memory, with the Jolli Memory footer intact.
can you add a note to use jolli memory when planning or writing code in this project when appropriate?

I'll add this as a note in the project's `CLAUDE.md`, which is the file Claude Code loads each session for project guidance. Let me first check whether one exists and what's in it.

No `CLAUDE.md` yet. I'll create one with a concise note. Since "jolli memory" here means the Jolli Memory tooling (the `jolli-recall`/`jolli-search` skills and `mcp__jollimemory__*` tools), I'll reference those concretely so the note is actionable.

Done. I created `/Users/jeverest/projects/snake-game/CLAUDE.md` with a note to use Jolli Memory when planning or writing code here.

I put it in `CLAUDE.md` rather than a memory file or a hook because:
- **`CLAUDE.md`** is loaded into context every session and is the right home for judgment-based project guidance ("when appropriate") — it applies to anyone working in the repo, not just your machine.
- A **hook** would be for a hard, deterministic trigger ("always run X before Y"); this is a "when relevant" guideline, so a hook would misfire.

The note points at the concrete tooling so it's actionable: `jolli-recall` before planning, `jolli-search`/`get_decision_timeline` for design questions, and `jolli-pr` for PR descriptions.

Want me to commit this? (You're on `feature/progression`.)
we will commit this later with this branch.  or should I crete a different branch and commit this on its own?

Separate branch. `CLAUDE.md` is a repo-wide workflow note — it has nothing to do with the progression feature, so bundling it in:

- adds unrelated noise to that PR's diff/review, and
- ties a trivial doc change to a feature that may take a while to land, when this could merge in minutes.

Since it's independent, it's also cleaner to branch it off `main` rather than off `feature/progression` (so the PR diff is just this one file, not this file + all the progression commits).

Want me to do that? I'd:
1. Stash/hold the new `CLAUDE.md` (it's currently untracked, so nothing to stash — it'll just come along).
2. `git checkout main && git checkout -b docs/claude-md-jolli-memory`
3. Commit `CLAUDE.md` there.

One check: is your local `main` up to date, or should I branch off `origin/main`? I'd branch off `origin/main` to be safe. Say the word and I'll run it.
yes, branch off origin/main and commit it

I'll fetch latest, branch off `origin/main`, and commit `CLAUDE.md` there.

On the new branch with `CLAUDE.md` restored as untracked. Now committing.
